framework_bugs.summary: U-D confirmation: no script advances current_phase after gate passes
framework_bugs.reproduction: After completing all phase1_architecture steps in a fresh project, run check-phase-gate.sh; .claude/phase-state.json .current_phase remains 0
framework_bugs.expected: current_phase advances to 2 (or some script provides --advance) after Phase 1 gate consistency confirmed
framework_bugs.actual: current_phase stayed at 0; only manual jq edit advanced it. No --advance-phase command exists in process-checklist.sh or check-phase-gate.sh.

tests.name: upgrade_command_exits_zero
tests.details: intake-wizard.sh --upgrade-deployment private_poc rejected by upgrade-project.sh validator (target must be personal|organizational); fell back to --upgrade-deployment organizational. That second invocation succeeded its first time but then aborted at 'Refreshing framework helper scripts' with `cp: scripts/pending-approval.sh ... are identical (not copied)`. RC=1 on the second/idempotent run because upgrade-project.sh runs `cp $SCRIPT_DIR/$helper scripts/$helper` and on macOS cp returns 1 when src and dst resolve to the same inode; combined with set -euo pipefail this terminates the script. CRITICAL regression.

tests.name: upgrade_writes_deployment_to_phase_state

tests.name: upgrade_to_private_poc_path_exists
tests.details: intake-wizard.sh routes --upgrade-deployment private_poc to upgrade-project.sh --deployment private_poc, but upgrade-project.sh validates deployment ∈ {personal, organizational} and rejects private_poc. There is no documented path for personal -> private_poc upgrade; --to-sponsored-poc, --to-production exist but no analogous --to-private-poc. Runbook target unreachable.

framework_bugs.summary: upgrade-project.sh aborts on idempotent helper-script refresh due to macOS cp same-file error under set -e
framework_bugs.reproduction: 1) cd into a fresh project with scripts/pending-approval.sh present (PR #17 already copies it during init). 2) bash scripts/upgrade-project.sh --deployment organizational. 3) Observe end-of-output: 'cp: scripts/pending-approval.sh and <abs path>/scripts/pending-approval.sh are identical (not copied)' followed by exit code 1.
framework_bugs.expected: Helper-script refresh is idempotent and the upgrade exits 0 even when scripts/pending-approval.sh is already up-to-date.
framework_bugs.actual: scripts/upgrade-project.sh:1473 runs `cp $SCRIPT_DIR/$helper scripts/$helper`. When SCRIPT_DIR is the project's own scripts/ dir (the normal invocation path: `bash scripts/upgrade-project.sh ...`), src and dst are the same file. macOS `cp` returns 1 with the 'are identical' message; set -euo pipefail terminates the script. Fix options: (a) skip cp when src and dst resolve to the same path, (b) `cp -p` is irrelevant — use `if ! cmp -s src dst; then cp ...; fi`, or (c) `cp src dst 2>/dev/null || true`. The first run succeeded only because the source file (the framework's copy via $FRAMEWORK/scripts/) and the destination are different inodes when invoked through the framework path; but when invoked through the project-local script (which the runbook does), they collide. Comment at line 1468 explicitly claims 'Idempotent: cp overwrites existing files identically' — that is false on macOS.

framework_bugs.summary: No upgrade path personal -> private_poc; intake-wizard --upgrade-deployment private_poc rejected by upgrade-project.sh
framework_bugs.reproduction: 1) bash scripts/intake-wizard.sh --upgrade-deployment private_poc. 2) intake-wizard exec's `bash scripts/upgrade-project.sh --deployment private_poc`. 3) upgrade-project validates deployment must be `personal` or `organizational` and exits 1 with: '[FAIL] Invalid target deployment: private_poc (must be: personal, organizational)'.
framework_bugs.expected: Either: (a) intake-wizard.sh --upgrade-deployment private_poc translates to the correct upgrade-project.sh invocation that adds POC governance + sets poc_mode=private_poc, OR (b) the runbook should target organizational instead of private_poc. The runbook explicitly instructs `--upgrade-deployment private_poc` so this is a script gap.
framework_bugs.actual: Documented upgrade paths in upgrade-project.sh --help cover: light->standard|full track upgrades; personal->organizational; private_poc->sponsored_poc; private_poc->production; sponsored_poc->production. No personal->private_poc transition exists, but the intake-wizard exposes the flag as if it does.

framework_bugs.summary: U-J confirmation: deployment field on .claude/phase-state.json not updated after upgrade
framework_bugs.reproduction: 1) Run upgrade-project.sh --deployment organizational from a fresh personal project. 2) Inspect .claude/phase-state.json: .deployment still "personal".
framework_bugs.expected: .deployment field updated to organizational; phase-state.json is the canonical state file per README and per PR #17's U-E fix that says upgrade-project.sh now reads poc_mode from phase-state.json.
framework_bugs.actual: CLAUDE.md, APPROVAL_LOG.md, and PROJECT_INTAKE.md correctly reflect organizational; phase-state.json's .deployment is stale at "personal"; .last_upgrade="2026-04-25" was added. Future scripts that read .deployment from phase-state.json will see the wrong value.

framework_bugs.summary: U-G confirmation: $FRAMEWORK/scripts/intake-wizard.sh treats $FRAMEWORK as PROJECT_ROOT; only project-local copy works
framework_bugs.reproduction: 1) cd to project dir. 2) bash $FRAMEWORK/scripts/intake-wizard.sh --upgrade-deployment private_poc. 3) Output: 'Error: PROJECT_INTAKE.md not found. Run this script from a Solo Orchestrator project directory.'
framework_bugs.expected: intake-wizard should use $PWD or $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR for PROJECT_ROOT, not $SCRIPT_DIR/.. (which collapses to $FRAMEWORK when invoked from the framework copy). Comment at intake-wizard.sh:16 acknowledges this is U-G deferred to Batch 3.
framework_bugs.actual: PROJECT_ROOT=$(cd $SCRIPT_DIR/.. && pwd) at line 20 always resolves to the framework root when the framework copy is invoked. The runbook's example uses `$FRAMEWORK/scripts/intake-wizard.sh` which therefore fails out unless replaced with the project-local copy `bash scripts/intake-wizard.sh`. Worked around by using project-local copy; not a regression but still open.

documentation_gaps.gap: Runbook Section F instructs `bash $FRAMEWORK/scripts/intake-wizard.sh --upgrade-deployment private_poc` but (a) the framework-relative invocation path triggers U-G unless workaround is used, and (b) `private_poc` is not a valid value for upgrade-project.sh --deployment. Need either a script-side translation (intake-wizard --upgrade-deployment private_poc -> upgrade-project --to-private-poc that adds POC governance + sets poc_mode) or a runbook correction (target organizational instead).

scripts/upgrade-project.sh --help documents personal->organizational but not personal->private_poc as an upgrade path; runbook implies this transition exists for upgrade-from=personal -> target=private_poc agents.

summary: Baseline init+phase1+phase2(1 feature) succeeded; PR #17 (U-F sentinel respected) and PR #19 (--non-interactive) confirmed working. Critical: upgrade-project.sh helper-refresh aborts on macOS due to cp same-file under set -e (RC=1). High: no personal->private_poc upgrade path exists; intake-wizard exposes the flag but upgrade-project rejects it. U-D, U-G, U-J confirmations recorded as Medium.
